S.text: Established patient for annual exam with new-onset symptoms.
Reports new floaters (cobweb-like) OD for ~1 week with occasional temporal flashes.
Denies curtain or shadow over the vision.

O.text: Pupils reactive, no afferent pupillary defect; confrontation fields full to finger count OU.
Manifest refraction OD -6.50 -1.25 x 100, VA cc 20/25 OD.
Manifest refraction OS -6.75 -1.00 x 080, VA cc 20/20 OS; high myopia OU.
Slit lamp: lids and conjunctiva quiet, corneas clear, anterior chambers deep, early lens changes OU.
Dilated fundus OD: optic nerve healthy C/D 0.3; posterior vitreous detachment; superotemporal horseshoe retinal tear with surrounding fluid.
Dilated fundus OS unremarkable: C/D 0.3, macula flat, periphery intact.

A.text: Horseshoe retinal tear OD with surrounding fluid and PVD — sight-threatening, requires urgent treatment.

P.text: Urgent referral to retina specialist for laser retinopexy OD; office to coordinate same-day/next-day appointment.
Activity precautions: avoid heavy lifting and sudden head movements; ER precautions for a visual field curtain.
Defer finalizing spectacle Rx change until retinal issue is managed.
